steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
“In contrast” establishes opposing artistic strategies.

Massey's Abstract Expressionism renders Indigenous identity invisible—viewers cannot detect it from the work's visual attributes.

Earles constructs abstraction from traditional tribal motifs—the opposite source.

These opposite approaches must produce opposite effects.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content:
Choice A states that abstraction in Earles's work renders Indigenous identity “more legible”.

This is the logical effect when abstraction is grounded in tribal cultural motifs—identity becomes visible and recognizable within the abstract form.

The other three choices either misstate Earles's source material, discuss Abstract Expressionism's dominance rather than legibility, or focus on Untitled.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
